message:
fix(render): clarify generation-tracking variable names in GraphemePool

Rename shadowed 'gen' variables to more descriptive names throughout the
GraphemePool implementation to prevent confusion between the generation
parameter and the looked-up slot generation:

- cell.rs proptest: gen -> generation (matches GraphemeId::new parameter)
- grapheme_pool.rs get/retain/release/refcount: gen -> slot_gen (makes
  clear this is the generation stored in the slot, not the query param)

Also updates beads issue tracker with latest metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3nkw2","title":"Markdown Integration: Mermaid Fence Rendering","description":"Background
- Markdown renderer currently shows raw Mermaid code fences.

Scope
- Detect ```mermaid fences in MarkdownRenderer.
- Parse Mermaid content and render via TME pipeline.
- Apply init directives and config overrides where allowed.
- Apply link policy and render hyperlinks/footnotes when enabled.
- On parse/layout errors, render structured error panel (with location) and optional raw snippet.
- Feature flag to enable/disable Mermaid rendering.
- Log render outcomes to JSONL (success/error, size, tier, warnings).

Considerations
- Rendering must respect available width/height for the panel.
- Errors must not crash or poison caches.

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests for Mermaid rendering in Markdown screen.
- Error-panel snapshot tests for malformed Mermaid.

Success Criteria
- Mermaid fences display diagrams, not raw code.
- Errors render as readable panels with location  {"id":"bd-3nohm","title":"Fix lexer byte-to-char casts for non-ASCII UTF-8 support","description":"The Mermaid lexer uses 'b as char' casts at 6 locations which incorrectly handle non-ASCII bytes. Multi-byte UTF-8 characters (CJK node names, emoji) get split into separate invalid Punct tokens. Fix: decode full UTF-8 chars and handle non-ASCII in identifier lexing.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:14:42.986648885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:26:03.548236676Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:19:12.269990645Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":788,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will fix the 6 byte-to-char cast locations in the Mermaid lexer to properly handle non-ASCII UTF-8 characters.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:18:00Z"},{"id":789,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed lexer byte-to-char casts: added decode_current_char()/advance_char() for UTF-8, updated is_ident_start/is_ident_continue for Unicode letters, fixed next_token/lex_identifier/lex_arrow_or_punct to branch on ASCII vs non-ASCII. Added tokenize_non_ascii_identifiers test. All 402 mermaid tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:19:08Z"},{"id":790,"issue_id":"bd-3nohm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear: Fixed. The core bug (non-ASCII bytes split into separate Punct tokens) was already fixed by a previous agent. I additionally:
1. Fixed column tracking in lex_string() and lex_comment_or_directive() — non-ASCII chars inside strings/comments now advance by 1 column instead of N bytes, via new advance_byte_or_char() helper
2. Added 6 comprehensive non-ASCII tests: CJK identifiers, CJK strings, emoji strings, emoji as Punct, CJK comments, and span column counting verification
3. Fixed pre-existing compilation errors: added missing 'constraints' field to MermaidDiagramIr constructors in mermaid.rs, dot_parser.rs, and diagram_layout_bench.rs
All 486 ftui-extras tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:26:03Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3o1l","title":"Text Effects: Mirror / Reflection","description":"## Background
Mirror/reflection creates a classic 'glossy' text effect with a vertically flipped copy below. Works especially well for titles using AsciiArtText, terminal-appropriate since it uses vertical space.

## Implementation

Add Reflection struct (separate from TextEffect for flexibility):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Reflection {
    pub gap: u16,            // Rows between text and reflection (0-3 typical)
    pub opacity_start: f64,  // Top of reflection (0.3-0.5 typical)
    pub opacity_end: f64,    // Bottom of reflection (0.0 typical)
    pub height_ratio: f64,   // 0.0-1.0, how much of text height to reflect
    pub wave: f64,           // 0.0=perfect mirror, 1.0=wavy water effect
}

impl Default for Reflection {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            gap: 1,
            opacity_start: 0.4,
            opacity_end: 0.0,
            height_ratio: 0.5,  // Reflect bottom half of text
            wave: 0.0,
        }
    }
}
```

### Usage with StyledText:
```rust
// Only works with multi-line text (AsciiArtText or actual multi-line)
let art = AsciiArtText::new("TITLE", AsciiArtStyle::Block);
StyledText::from_ascii_art(art)
    .reflection(Reflection::default())
    .effect(TextEffect::HorizontalGradient { ... })
```

### Single-Line Behavior:
For single-line text, reflection is a no-op (nothing to flip vertically). Use AsciiArtText for reflections on titles.

### Rendering:
```rust
fn render_with_reflection(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
    // 1. Render main text
    self.render_text(main_area, frame);
    
    // 2. Calculate reflection area
    let reflect_height = (self.text_height as f64 * self.reflection.height_ratio) as u16;
    let reflect_y = main_area.bottom() + self.reflection.gap;
    
    // 3. Render flipped text with gradient opacity
    for row in 0..reflect_height {
        let source_row = self.text_height - 1 - row;  // Flip vertically
        let opacity = lerp(self.reflection.opacity_start, 
                          self.reflection.opacity_end,
                          row as f64 / reflect_height as f64);
        
        let wave_offset = if self.reflection.wave > 0.0 {
            (row as f64 * 0.5 + self.time * 2.0).sin() * self.reflection.wave
        } else { 0.0 };
        
        self.render_row_with_opacity(source_row, reflect_y + row, opacity, wave_offset, frame);
    }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_reflection_single_line_noop: No reflection for 1-row text
- test_reflection_multiline_flips: Rows rendered in reverse order
- test_reflection_gap_applied: Correct spacing between text and reflection
- test_reflection_opacity_gradient: Top brighter, bottom fainter
- test_reflection_height_ratio: Only reflects specified portion
- test_reflection_wave_offsets: Horizontal shimmer with wave > 0
- test_reflection_inherits_effects: Gradient text = gradient reflection

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot 5-row AsciiArt 'HELLO' with reflection
- Snapshot with wave=0.5 showing water ripple effect
- Snapshot gradient text with reflection (both should be gradient)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Mirror / Reflection
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","notes":"Enhanced Reflection struct: replaced height with gap/height_ratio/wave. Added reflected_rows(), wave shimmer, gap spacing. 5 new tests + 3 updated.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:07:09.767617641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:24.700806595Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:24.700773683Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["text-effects"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o1l","depends_on_id":"bd-2bzl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig","title":"Mermaid Mega Showcase Screen (interactive layout lab)","description":"Purpose:
- Detailed breakdown for bd-2nkmi (Mermaid Showcase Super Screen). This epic decomposes the work into granular, testable tasks.

Goal:
- Add a brand-new, over-the-top, super-interactive Mermaid demo screen that showcases every Mermaid feature we already support, plus live layout recomputation under size/complexity changes.

Why:
- The demo showcase is the primary product lens; this screen should prove correctness, determinism, and performance of our Mermaid pipeline under stress.
- We need a single place to show complex diagrams, layout recalculation speed, and debug instrumentation.

Scope:
- New screen in ftui-demo-showcase with rich UI, multiple diagram families, live controls, and performance panels.
- Curated + parameterized samples, layout/route/debug overlays, and a polished help/legend panel.
- Unit tests, snapshot tests, and E2E scripts with explicit logging.

Non-goals:
- Rewriting the Mermaid parser or introducing new diagram syntax not already supported.

Acceptance:
- A dedicated screen is selectable in the demo showcase and is stable across multiple sizes.
- Users can change sample, size, layout knobs, and see recomputation timings and layout stability.
- Tests provide deterministic coverage of samples, layout behavior, and input handling.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:30:50.484372892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:05:32.826589527Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T03:05:32.826565713Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","mermaid","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig","depends_on_id":"bd-2nkmi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:37:03.069977897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":827,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltHollow: All 30 sub-beads closed. Epic complete. The Mermaid Mega Showcase Screen is fully implemented with: 70+ samples across all diagram types, parametric generator, performance sweep, comparison mode, zoom/pan, auto-scale, auto-fit, search, debug overlays, help overlay, structured JSONL telemetry, color-coded metrics panel, and comprehensive E2E + unit tests (236+ tests).","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:05:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oaig.1","title":"Mega screen feature matrix + scope map","description":"Goal:
- Inventory Mermaid features already implemented and map them to concrete demo coverage.

Deliverables:
- A written feature matrix in-code (doc comment or static data) that lists: diagram families, supported syntax features, and which sample demonstrates each feature.
- Explicit gaps/unknowns captured as TODOs in the matrix.
- Coverage counts per family (features covered vs total supported).

Considerations:
- Focus on existing capabilities only; do not invent unsupported syntax.
- Include stress/perf features (large graphs, dense edge labels, multi-line nodes).
- Matrix must be used by sample registry; this prevents samples from drifting away from stated feature coverage.

Acceptance:
- Matrix is easy to update and referenced by the mega screen sample registry.
- Every planned sample has a declared purpose and linked feature coverage.
- A small unit test validates that each sample references only features present in the matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:11.587982916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:47:42.798825967Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:44:16.523237899Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","mermaid","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:11.587982916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":748,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented feature matrix as FEATURE_MATRIX const in mermaid.rs with 40 entries covering all 8 diagram types plus cross-cutting features. Added 8 tests for matrix completeness, type coverage, palette roundtrip, and config hash. 450 tests passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:44:12Z"},{"id":751,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SilentBrook: Completed. Added KNOWN_FEATURE_TAGS (35 tags) and FEATURE_GAPS (13 documented gaps) static arrays. Added 4 matrix validation tests ensuring tag consistency. 100 mermaid tests passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:48:35Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-fp38v.1","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Evidence Ledger","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Evidence Ledger (EV=10)

### Scope
Full integration test rendering with evidence emission enabled across all 7 Bayesian decision points. Verifies schema compliance, no missing fields, and correct evidence structure for every decision.

### Test Scenario
1. Enable evidence ledger emission for all 7 decision points:
   a. Diff strategy selection (incremental vs full)
   b. Resize coalescing (coalesce vs immediate)
   c. Frame budget management (admit vs defer)
   d. Degradation level selection (normal/reduced/minimal)
   e. VOI (value of information) for measurement decisions
   f. Hint ranking for command palette
   g. Command palette scoring
2. Render demo screens that exercise each decision point.
3. For diff strategy: alternate between small edits (incremental) and full redraws.
4. For resize: send rapid resize events.
5. For frame budget: inject artificial load spikes.
6. For degradation: gradually increase frame times.
7. Collect all JSONL evidence entries and validate schema.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each decision point emits a unified evidence entry:
```json
{
  "event": "evidence_ledger",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "decision_point": "diff_strategy|resize_coalesce|frame_budget|degradation|voi|hint_rank|cmd_score",
  "log_posterior": f64,
  "top_evidence": [
    { "term": "string", "bayes_factor": f64, "direction": "for|against" }
  ],
  "action_chosen": "string",
  "loss_avoided": f64,
  "confidence_interval": { "lower": f64, "upper": f64, "level": f64 },
  "alternatives_considered": u32,
  "frame_id": u64
}
```

### Success Criteria
- All 7 decision points emit at least one evidence entry during the test run.
- Every evidence entry has all required fields (no nulls in required fields).
- top_evidence array has exactly 3 entries per decision (top-3 evidence terms).
- bayes_factor values are positive and finite (no NaN/Inf).
- confidence_interval.lower < confidence_interval.upper.
- Schema validates against JSON Schema definition.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is parseable and every line deserializes without error.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:34.466502948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T18:41:38.524739552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fp38v.1","depends_on_id":"bd-6i8zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:27:07.857688982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-fp38v.1","depends_on_id":"bd-fp38v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:34.466502948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-fq968","title":"Mermaid init directives: snapshots + E2E JSONL logging","description":"Goal: once TME rendering exists, add snapshot coverage for init directives (theme + direction) and E2E JSONL logs including init_config_hash + warnings.\n\nScope\n- Add render snapshots that demonstrate themeVariables + flowchart.direction effects on diagrams.\n- Add PTY E2E flow covering init directives; JSONL should include init_config_hash and warnings.\n- Ensure deterministic seeds and stable hashes.\n\nNotes\n- Blocked until Mermaid TME renderer + terminal renderer is implemented (bd-hudcn/bd-12o4t).","notes":"Prep plan (blocked on bd-hudcn/bd-12o4t):
- Snapshot cases: flowchart w/ init {theme: 'dark' or 'base', themeVariables:{primaryColor,lineColor}} + flowchart.direction (TB/LR) to verify direction + theme overrides.
- Sizes: 80x24 + 120x40; deterministic seed/time via demo determinism fixtures (ftui-demo-showcase/src/determinism.rs).
- E2E: extend demo sweep script to run mermaid fixture with init directives; JSONL fields include init_config_hash, init_theme, init_theme_vars count, init_warnings[], flow_direction, size, mode, checksum.
- Failure mode: if renderer not available, test should skip with clear message (avoid compatibility shims in code path).

Recon:
- Mermaid init directive fixture: crates/ftui-extras/tests/fixtures/mermaid/graph_init_directive.mmd.
- Mermaid JSONL events already emitted: mermaid_prepare includes init_config_hash (crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs).
- Demo sweep script: tests/e2e/scripts/test_demo_showcase_sweep.sh (JSONL helpers in tests/e2e/lib/logging.sh).

Progress (2026-02-05):
- Added JSONL tests for mermaid_prepare + mermaid_guard in crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs (temp log helper; no file deletion).
- Fixed layout wiring: compute_node_sizes + pass cluster_map into minimize_crossings; allow clippy::too_many_arguments on multi_rank_waypoints in crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_layout.rs.
- Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (green).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDesert","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:23:39.515062209Z","created_by":"GoldWaterfall","updated_at":"2026-02-08T23:03:48.223899436Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T23:03:48.223879509Z","close_reason":"Completed: init-directive snapshots + PTY/E2E mermaid_prepare JSONL (init hash + warnings)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","mermaid","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fq968","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:23:39.515062209Z","created_by":"GoldWaterfall","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-fq968","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:23:39.515062209Z","created_by":"GoldWaterfall","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":998,"issue_id":"bd-fq968","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting bd-fq968: will add targeted init-directive snapshots (themeVariables + direction) and extend PTY/E2E JSONL assertions to include init_config_hash + warnings for init-directive samples.","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:15:44Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gajnk","title":"epic(ftui): Universal Split-Pane Workspace (terminal + web)","description":"Background\n- We need production-grade, click-drag resizable panes in both native terminal and web/wasm runtimes, with identical behavior semantics and first-class performance.\n- Existing layout and hit-testing primitives are strong, but there is no unified pane workspace subsystem that guarantees parity between hosts.\n\nNorth-Star Outcome\n- A single shared pane engine powers both runtimes.\n- Users can split, resize, collapse, reorder, and persist complex pane workspaces with mouse/touch/keyboard parity.\n- Interaction remains deterministic, smooth, and robust under heavy UI load.\n\nWhy this matters\n- Pane workspaces are the core interaction model for advanced TUIs and browser-hosted shells.\n- Delivering this well improves usability, discoverability, and composability across all advanced screens/tools.\n\nDesign Principles\n1. One canonical pane model in Rust; host adapters must not fork behavior.\n2. Deterministic math and event handling for replay/debug.\n3. Bounded cost per frame and bounded memory growth.\n4. Inline-mode safety in terminal (scrollback-preserving defaults).\n5. Accessibility and keyboard parity are built-in, not bolted on.\n\nHard Invariants\n- Split tree remains acyclic and structurally valid after every operation.\n- Min/max constraints are always respected after resize.\n- Drag semantics are deterministic for equivalent event streams.\n- Rendering cannot cause terminal state corruption or web pointer deadlocks.\n\nScope\n- Shared pane core (split tree, constraints, operations, history).\n- Shared interaction contract (drag lifecycle, snapping, thresholds).\n- Terminal adapter implementation.\n- Web/wasm adapter implementation.\n- UX polish (handles, overlays, discoverability).\n- Persistence and recovery.\n- Full testing matrix (unit/property/PTY/browser perf+soak).\n\nOut of Scope\n- Arbitrary floating windows beyond the split-tree model (separate future epic).\n- Remote multi-user collaborative editing of pane layouts (future epic).\n\nSuccess Metrics\n- 60+ FPS sustained on modern web hardware for representative demos.\n- Terminal drag updates feel immediate and stable (no jitter/flicker).\n- Parity scorecard: terminal vs web behavior diffs are empty for canonical scenarios.\n- Deterministic replay reproduces  {"id":"bd-gpe","title":"Implement TextInput widget (agent harness essential)","description":"# Implement TextInput Widget (Agent Harness Essential)

## Background & Strategic Importance
TextInput is explicitly called out in the plan (Section 12.4) as an "agent harness essential" widget. It provides single-line text input for command prompts, search boxes, and user input fields. This is critical for Claude Code / Codex-style agent harness UIs where users need to type commands and queries.

## Why Elevated Priority (P1)
The plan states under "H) Widgets (v1 essentials)":
- "Text input / prompt line" is a REQUIRED widget for v1
- Agent harnesses need a way for users to type input
- This is part of the "minimum harness primitives" contract
- Without TextInput, the Agent Harness reference app (bd-2kj) cannot be built

---

## Technical Design

### Core Data Structure

```rust
use tracing::{debug, trace, instrument};
use unicode_segmentation::UnicodeSegmentation;

/// Single-line text input widget with cursor, selection, and editing operations.
///
/// # Design Rationale
/// - String storage is UTF-8 with byte-offset cursor (standard Rust)
/// - All cursor operations are grapheme-cluster aware (not byte or char)
/// - Selection uses Option<anchor> pattern for shift+arrow selection
/// - Blink state is external (runtime tick) for consistency
pub struct TextInput {
    /// Current text content (UTF-8)
    value: String,
    /// Cursor position as grapheme index (not byte offset!)
    cursor: usize,
    /// Selection anchor as grapheme index, None if no selection
    selection_anchor: Option<usize>,
    /// Placeholder text shown when empty
    placeholder: Option<String>,
    /// Password mode - mask characters
    masked: bool,
    /// Mask character for password mode (default: '•')
    mask_char: char,
    /// Horizontal scroll offset (grapheme index) for long text
    scroll_offset: usize,
    /// Maximum length in graphemes (None = unlimited)
    max_length: Option<usize>,
    /// Style for text
    style: Style,
    /// Style for cursor (typically reverse video)
    cursor_style: Style,
    /// Style for placeholder
    placeholder_style: Style,
    /// Style for selection highlight
    selection_style: Style,
}

/// State tracking for stateful widget pattern
pub struct TextInputState {
    /// Whether cursor is currently visible (blink state)
    cursor_visible: bool,
    /// Whether input is focused
    focused: bool,
}
```

### Public API

```rust
impl TextInput {
    /// Create empty text input.
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        debug!("Creating TextInput");
        Self {
            value: String::new(),
            cursor: 0,
            selection_anchor: None,
            placeholder: None,
            masked: false,
            mask_char: '•',
            scroll_offset: 0,
            max_length: None,
            style: Style::default(),
            cursor_style: Style::default().add_modifier(Modifier::REVERSED),
            placeholder_style: Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
            selection_style: Style::default().bg(Color::Blue),
        }
    }

    // --- Value access ---

    /// Get current value.
    pub fn value(&self) -> &str { &self.value }

    /// Set value, clamping cursor to valid position.
    pub fn set_value(&mut self, value: impl Into<String>) {
        self.value = value.into();
        let max = self.grapheme_count();
        self.cursor = self.cursor.min(max);
        self.selection_anchor = None;
        debug!(len = self.value.len(), cursor = self.cursor, "Value set");
    }

    // --- Builder methods ---

    pub fn placeholder(mut self, text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.placeholder = Some(text.into());
        self
    }

    pub fn masked(mut self) -> Self {
        self.masked = true;
        self
    }

    pub fn mask_char(mut self, c: char) -> Self {
        self.mask_char = c;
        self
    }

    pub fn max_length(mut self, len: usize) -> Self {
        self.max_length = Some(len);
        self
    }

    // --- Cursor movement ---

    /// Move cursor left one grapheme cluster.
    #[instrument(skip(self), fields(cursor = self.cursor))]
    pub fn move_cursor_left(&mut self) {
        if self.cursor > 0 {
            self.cursor -= 1;
            self.selection_anchor = None;
            trace!(new_cursor = self.cursor, "Cursor left");
        }
    }

    /// Move cursor right one grapheme cluster.
    pub fn move_cursor_right(&mut self) {
        let max = self.grapheme_count();
        if self.cursor < max {
            self.cursor += 1;
            self.selection_anchor = None;
            trace!(new_cursor = self.cursor, "Cursor right");
        }
    }

    /// Move cursor to start of line.
    pub fn move_cursor_start(&mut self) {
        self.cursor = 0;
        self.selection_anchor = None;
        debug!("Cursor to start");
    }

    /// Move cursor to end of line.
    pub fn move_cursor_end(&mut self) {
        self.cursor = self.grapheme_count();
        self.selection_anchor = None;
        debug!("Cursor to end");
    }

    /// Move cursor left one word (word = sequence of alphanumeric).
    pub fn move_cursor_word_left(&mut self) {
        // Skip any whitespace, then skip to start of word
        let graphemes: Vec<&str> = self.value.graphemes(true).collect();
        let mut pos = self.cursor;

        // Skip trailing whitespace
        while pos > 0 && graphemes.get(pos - 1).map_or(false, |g| g.chars().all(char::is_whitespace)) {
            pos -= 1;
        }
        // Skip word characters
        while pos > 0 && graphemes.get(pos - 1).map_or(false, |g| g.chars().any(char::is_alphanumeric)) {
            pos -= 1;
        }

        self.cursor = pos;
        self.selection_anchor = None;
        trace!(new_cursor = self.cursor, "Cursor word left");
    }

    /// Move cursor right one word.
    pub fn move_cursor_word_right(&mut self) {
        let graphemes: Vec<&str> = self.value.graphemes(true).collect();
        let max = graphemes.len();
        let mut pos = self.cursor;

        // Skip current word
        while pos < max && graphemes.get(pos).map_or(false, |g| g.chars().any(char::is_alphanumeric)) {
            pos += 1;
        }
        // Skip whitespace
        while pos < max && graphemes.get(pos).map_or(false, |g| g.chars().all(char::is_whitespace)) {
            pos += 1;
        }

        self.cursor = pos;
        self.selection_anchor = None;
        trace!(new_cursor = self.cursor, "Cursor word right");
    }

    // --- Editing operations ---

    /// Insert character at cursor position.
    #[instrument(skip(self), fields(cursor = self.cursor, char = %c))]
    pub fn insert_char(&mut self, c: char) {
        // Check max length
        if let Some(max) = self.max_length {
            if self.grapheme_count() >= max {
                trace!("Insert blocked: max_length reached");
                return;
            }
        }

        // Delete selection first if any
        self.delete_selection();

        // Find byte offset for cursor grapheme position
        let byte_offset = self.cursor_byte_offset();
        self.value.insert(byte_offset, c);
        self.cursor += 1;
        trace!(new_cursor = self.cursor, "Inserted char");
    }

    /// Delete character before cursor (backspace).
    pub fn delete_char_before(&mut self) {
        if self.selection_anchor.is_some() {
            self.delete_selection();
            return;
        }

        if self.cursor > 0 {
            let byte_start = self.grapheme_byte_offset(self.cursor - 1);
            let byte_end = self.grapheme_byte_offset(self.cursor);
            self.value.drain(byte_start..byte_end);
            self.cursor -= 1;
            trace!(cursor = self.cursor, "Deleted char before");
        }
    }

    /// Delete character after cursor (delete key).
    pub fn delete_char_after(&mut self) {
        if self.selection_anchor.is_some() {
            self.delete_selection();
            return;
        }

        let max = self.grapheme_count();
        if self.cursor < max {
            let byte_start = self.cursor_byte_offset();
            let byte_end = self.grapheme_byte_offset(self.cursor + 1);
            self.value.drain(byte_start..byte_end);
            trace!(cursor = self.cursor, "Deleted char after");
        }
    }

    /// Delete word before cursor (Ctrl+Backspace).
    pub fn delete_word_before(&mut self) {
        let old_cursor = self.cursor;
        self.move_cursor_word_left();
        let new_cursor = self.cursor;

        if new_cursor < old_cursor {
            let byte_start = self.grapheme_byte_offset(new_cursor);
            let byte_end = self.grapheme_byte_offset(old_cursor);
            self.value.drain(byte_start..byte_end);
            debug!(deleted = old_cursor - new_cursor, "Deleted word before");
        }
    }

    /// Clear all text.
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.value.clear();
        self.cursor = 0;
        self.selection_anchor = None;
        debug!("Cleared");
    }

    // --- Selection operations ---

    /// Select all text.
    pub fn select_all(&mut self) {
        self.selection_anchor = Some(0);
        self.cursor = self.grapheme_count();
        debug!("Selected all");
    }

    /// Get selected text, if any.
    pub fn selected_text(&self) -> Option<&str> {
        self.selection_anchor.map(|anchor| {
            let (start, end) = self.selection_range(anchor);
            let byte_start = self.grapheme_byte_offset(start);
            let byte_end = self.grapheme_byte_offset(end);
            &self.value[byte_start..byte_end]
        })
    }

    /// Delete selected text.
    pub fn delete_selection(&mut self) {
        if let Some(anchor) = self.selection_anchor.take() {
            let (start, end) = self.selection_range(anchor);
            let byte_start = self.grapheme_byte_offset(start);
            let byte_end = self.grapheme_byte_offset(end);
            self.value.drain(byte_start..byte_end);
            self.cursor = start;
            debug!(start, end, "Deleted selection");
        }
    }

    // --- Helper methods ---

    fn grapheme_count(&self) -> usize {
        self.value.graphemes(true).count()
    }

    fn cursor_byte_offset(&self) -> usize {
        self.grapheme_byte_offset(self.cursor)
    }

    fn grapheme_byte_offset(&self, grapheme_idx: usize) -> usize {
        self.value.grapheme_indices(true)
            .nth(grapheme_idx)
            .map(|(i, _)| i)
            .unwrap_or(self.value.len())
    }

    fn selection_range(&self, anchor: usize) -> (usize, usize) {
        if anchor <= self.cursor {
            (anchor, self.cursor)
        } else {
            (self.cursor, anchor)
        }
    }
}
```

### Rendering Implementation

```rust
impl StatefulWidget for TextInput {
    type State = TextInputState;

    #[instrument(skip(self, area, buf, state), fields(
        value_len = self.value.len(),
        cursor = self.cursor,
        has_selection = self.selection_anchor.is_some()
    ))]
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, state: &mut Self::State) {
        if area.width == 0 || area.height == 0 {
            return;
        }

        // Determine display text (actual or masked)
        let display_text: String = if self.masked {
            std::iter::repeat(self.mask_char)
                .take(self.grapheme_count())
                .collect()
        } else if self.value.is_empty() {
            self.placeholder.clone().unwrap_or_default()
        } else {
            self.value.clone()
        };

        // Calculate horizontal scroll for long text
        let visible_width = area.width as usize;
        let display_graphemes: Vec<&str> = display_text.graphemes(true).collect();

        // Ensure cursor is visible by adjusting scroll_offset
        // ... scroll calculation logic ...

        // Render visible portion
        let mut x = area.x;
        for (i, grapheme) in display_graphemes.iter().skip(self.scroll_offset).enumerate() {
            if x >= area.x + area.width {
                break;
            }

            let style = if self.selection_anchor.is_some()
                && self.is_in_selection(self.scroll_offset + i)
            {
                self.selection_style
            } else if self.value.is_empty() {
                self.placeholder_style
            } else {
                self.style
            };

            let grapheme_width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(*grapheme) as u16;
            buf.set_string(x, area.y, grapheme, style);
            x += grapheme_width;
        }

        // Render cursor if focused and visible
        if state.focused && state.cursor_visible {
            let cursor_x = area.x + self.cursor_display_x(self.scroll_offset) as u16;
            if cursor_x < area.x + area.width {
                let cursor_char = display_graphemes
                    .get(self.cursor)
                    .copied()
                    .unwrap_or(" ");
                buf.set_string(cursor_x, area.y, cursor_char, self.cursor_style);
            }
        }

        trace!(scroll_offset = self.scroll_offset, "Rendered TextInput");
    }
}
```

### Cursor Position for Frame

```rust
impl TextInput {
    /// Get the cursor position for Frame::set_cursor().
    /// Returns (x, y) relative to render area.
    pub fn cursor_position(&self, area: Rect) -> (u16, u16) {
        let x = area.x + self.cursor_display_x(self.scroll_offset) as u16;
        let y = area.y;
        (x.min(area.x + area.width - 1), y)
    }
}

// In parent view function:
// frame.set_cursor(input.cursor_position(input_area));
```

## Event Handling Pattern

```rust
// In parent Model::update()
fn handle_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> Cmd<Msg> {
    match key.code {
        KeyCode::Char(c) if !key.modifiers.contains(Modifiers::CTRL) => {
            self.input.insert_char(c);
        }
        KeyCode::Backspace => {
            if key.modifiers.contains(Modifiers::CTRL) {
                self.input.delete_word_before();
            } else {
                self.input.delete_char_before();
            }
        }
        KeyCode::Delete => self.input.delete_char_after(),
        KeyCode::Left => {
            if key.modifiers.contains(Modifiers::CTRL) {
                self.input.move_cursor_word_left();
            } else {
                self.input.move_cursor_left();
            }
        }
        KeyCode::Right => {
            if key.modifiers.contains(Modifiers::CTRL) {
                self.input.move_cursor_word_right();
            } else {
                self.input.move_cursor_right();
            }
        }
        KeyCode::Home => self.input.move_cursor_start(),
        KeyCode::End => self.input.move_cursor_end(),
        KeyCode::Enter => return Cmd::msg(Msg::Submit(self.input.value().to_string())),
        _ => {}
    }
    Cmd::none()
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use tracing_test::traced_test;

    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_empty_input_renders_placeholder() {
        let input = TextInput::new().placeholder("Enter text...");
        assert!(input.value().is_empty());
        // Render should show placeholder
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_insert_char() {
        let mut input = TextInput::new();
        input.insert_char('a');
        input.insert_char('b');
        input.insert_char('c');
        assert_eq!(input.value(), "abc");
        assert_eq!(input.cursor, 3);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_cursor_movement() {
        let mut input = TextInput::new();
        input.set_value("hello world");
        input.move_cursor_end();
        assert_eq!(input.cursor, 11);
        input.move_cursor_left();
        assert_eq!(input.cursor, 10);
        input.move_cursor_start();
        assert_eq!(input.cursor, 0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_word_movement() {
        let mut input = TextInput::new();
        input.set_value("hello world test");
        input.move_cursor_end();
        input.move_cursor_word_left();
        assert_eq!(input.cursor, 12); // Before "test"
        input.move_cursor_word_left();
        assert_eq!(input.cursor, 6); // Before "world"
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_backspace_delete() {
        let mut input = TextInput::new();
        input.set_value("hello");
        input.move_cursor_end();
        input.delete_char_before();
        assert_eq!(input.value(), "hell");
        input.move_cursor_start();
        input.delete_char_after();
        assert_eq!(input.value(), "ell");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_unicode_grapheme_handling() {
        let mut input = TextInput::new();
        // Emoji with skin tone modifier (single grapheme, multiple code points)
        input.insert_char('👋');
        input.insert_char('🏽');
        // This is actually two graphemes, but testing the concept
        assert_eq!(input.cursor, 2);

        // ZWJ sequence: family emoji
        input.clear();
        input.set_value("👨‍👩‍👧");  // Single grapheme cluster
        assert_eq!(input.grapheme_count(), 1);
        input.move_cursor_right();
        assert_eq!(input.cursor, 1);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_max_length() {
        let mut input = TextInput::new().max_length(5);
        for c in "abcdefgh".chars() {
            input.insert_char(c);
        }
        assert_eq!(input.value(), "abcde"); // Only first 5
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_password_masking() {
        let input = TextInput::new().masked().set_value("secret");
        // When rendered, should show "••••••" instead of "secret"
        assert_eq!(input.value(), "secret"); // Internal value unchanged
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_selection() {
        let mut input = TextInput::new();
        input.set_value("hello world");
        input.select_all();
        assert_eq!(input.selected_text(), Some("hello world"));
        input.delete_selection();
        assert!(input.value().is_empty());
    }
}
```

### Performance Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_long_input_performance() {
    let mut input = TextInput::new();
    let long_text = "x".repeat(10_000);
    input.set_value(&long_text);

    let start = Instant::now();
    for _ in 0..1000 {
        input.move_cursor_right();
        input.move_cursor_left();
    }
    assert!(start.elapsed().as_millis() < 100, "Cursor ops should be fast");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional
- [ ] Text insertion at cursor position
- [ ] Cursor movement (left, right, word-left, word-right, home, end)
- [ ] Backspace and delete (char and word)
- [ ] Selection with shift+arrow (stretch goal)
- [ ] Placeholder shown when empty
- [ ] Password mode with masking
- [ ] Max length enforcement
- [ ] Horizontal scroll for long text

### Unicode
- [ ] Grapheme-cluster aware cursor (not byte or char)
- [ ] Wide character handling (CJK, emoji)
- [ ] ZWJ sequences treated as single unit

### Integration
- [ ] cursor_position() returns correct Frame cursor coords
- [ ] Works with runtime event loop
- [ ] Cursor blinks (external state via TextInputState)

### Logging
- [ ] debug! for major operations (set_value, clear, select_all)
- [ ] trace! for cursor movement, char insertion
- [ ] instrument on render() with key fields
